feedback.text: Hello, Please consider adding another point to your digital act: developers on purpose destroying electronic entertainment (video games) that customers bought. Video game consumers are getting the short stick of the greedy corporations. Some live-service games don't even survive for a month, but have to be bought for the illusion of sustained service. This practice is wrong and more checks have to be put in place, so that people who bought a product would at least know it's expiry date. So please consider adding "stop developers from killing video games" in the Digital Fairness act. We live in a digital world and it should be fair for all. Thank you
